title: /etc/hosts duplicated every time after container restarted
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

`/etc/hosts` duplicated every time when container restarted

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

```sh
sudo podman pod create --name demo-pod
sudo podman run --name=demo-container --detach --restart=always --pod=demo-pod nginx
sudo podman exec -it demo-container cat /etc/hosts | grep demo-container | wc
# get 1

sudo podman restart demo-container
sudo podman exec -it demo-container cat /etc/hosts | grep demo-container | wc
# get 2

sudo podman restart demo-container
sudo podman exec -it demo-container cat /etc/hosts | grep demo-container | wc
# get 3
```

**Describe the results you received:**

`/etc/hosts` keep duplicating `127.0.1.1 demo-pod demo-container` after I restart `demo-container`

**Describe the results you expected:**

`127.0.1.1 demo-pod demo-container` should not duplicated in `/etc/hosts`

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      2.2.1
API Version:  2.1.0
Go Version:   go1.15.6
Git Commit:   a0d478edea7f775b7ce32f8eb1a01e75374486cb
Built:        Wed Dec  9 04:48:23 2020
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```
